title: RefluXFS: A Linux Kernel Local Privilege Escalation to Root in XFS (CVE-2026-64600)

content_html:
Executive summary

Qualys Threat Research Unit (TRU) identified CVE-2026-64600, a race condition in the Linux kernel’s XFS filesystem copy-on-write path. An attacker with an ordinary local account can exploit this race condition to overwrite protected files on disk and gain host root privileges on affected systems, including deployments running SELinux in Enforcing mode.

This discovery emerged from a structured research initiative between Qualys and Anthropic, where we integrated Claude Mythos Preview into our manual audit workflow to accelerate our research while maintaining strict human oversight. 

Using this vulnerability, a process running as an ordinary, unprivileged user can trigger the flaw and gain the ability to overwrite any readable file on an XFS volume at the block layer. This primitive converts directly into host root privileges. Exploitation is highly reliable and leaves no kernel log output. The vulnerability affects any Linux distribution that ships an XFS root filesystem with reflink enabled. This includes default installations of major enterprise platforms such as RHEL, Oracle Linux, Amazon Linux, and Fedora.

This vulnerability has been present since kernel version 4.11 (circa 2017) and, based on analysis with Qualys CyberSecurity Asset Management, potentially impacts over 16.4 million systems worldwide, requiring immediate attention due to its high exploitability and lack of kernel logging.

Immediate kernel patching is recommended to neutralize this vulnerability. Exploitation succeeds consistently under standard hardening settings, and the on-disk modification survives a system reboot.

Vendor-fixed kernels are now available and being backported to enterprise distributions. Organizations should prioritize patching exposed and multi-tenant systems and ensure a reboot to verify the update. As of now, there are no reliable or practical mitigations or temporary configuration changes available.

We rate this as an emergency priority because exploitation could begin from ordinary local privileges. The vulnerability is present in standard enterprise kernel builds, and a successful exploitation provides host root. The exploitation works under common kernel hardening settings, and fixed kernels are available.

RefluXFS is less about what AI can do than how it’s used responsibly. Capable models have been broadly available for some time; direction, not capability, was the constraint. The lesson isn’t that AI can be aimed at software to produce exploits; it’s that AI, paired with expert judgment, helps defenders find and close serious flaws before they’re abused.

Discovery process and research methodology

For this research, we tasked Claude Mythos Preview with hunting for a Dirty COW–style race condition in the Linux kernel, iteratively refining our prompts to narrow its focus toward race conditions in the core memory-management and filesystem directories. After several iterations, the model identified a race condition in the XFS filesystem and generated a functional proof-of-concept local privilege escalation. Our security researchers then took over: we reviewed the model’s reasoning, reproduced the exploit, and independently verified every technical claim before coordinating disclosure with upstream maintainers. The model also produced an initial draft of the advisory, which our team validated and corrected against our own testing. This human-validated, AI-accelerated approach let us surface a complex kernel race condition while holding to the strict accuracy and responsible-disclosure standards expected; every finding here cleared the same evidence bar we apply to any Qualys security advisory.

Find out more about Qualys’ participation in Anthropic’s Project Glasswing.

Understanding the Potential Impact of the RefluXFS Vulnerability

An unprivileged local user can overwrite the on-disk contents of any readable file on a reflink-enabled XFS volume. Our proof-of-concept reliably gains host root privileges by surgically modifying /etc/passwd or SUID-root binaries. Changes persist across reboots, leave no kernel log output, and bypass standard file metadata checks.

While CVE-2026-64600 itself is strictly local, successful exploitation on a compromised host could enable attackers to establish persistence, manipulate credentials, or facilitate lateral movement within a network.

Watch a RefluXFS Proof-of-concept Demonstration

The following proof-of-concept shown in the video below shows the RefluXFS vulnerability on a default RHEL 10.2 deployment. Starting as an unprivileged local user with no administrative rights, the exploit triggers the race condition to silently overwrite a protected system file at the block layer. Within seconds, the root account’s password protection is stripped, granting immediate, passwordless root access. The modification persists across reboots and leaves no kernel log artifacts.

RefluXFS PoC execution on RHEL 10.2. Unprivileged user escalates to root by exploiting the XFS reflink race condition.

Affected Systems & Prerequisites

Vulnerability Scope & Impact:

The RefluXFS vulnerability affects systems meeting all three of the following criteria:

  1. Kernel Version: Running Linux kernel v4.11 or later (2017) without the specific security patch.
  2. Filesystem Configuration: Utilizing an XFS filesystem with reflink=1 in its superblock.
  3. File Structure: The filesystem must contain both a high-value target (a root-owned configuration file or SUID-root binary) and a directory writable by an unprivileged local user.

Affected Distributions:

This vulnerability has been present in every mainline and stable kernel since 2017. It requires no special capabilities or non-default configurations. The distributions listed below are those confirmed to be affected; this is not an exhaustive list, and other distributions, particularly those derived from RHEL, are likely vulnerable as well:

  • RHEL 8, 9, and 10
  • CentOS Stream 8, 9, and 10
  • Oracle Linux 8, 9, and 10
  • Rocky and AlmaLinux 8, 9, 10
  • CloudLinux 8, 9, and 10
  • Amazon Linux 2023 and Amazon Linux 2 AMIs from December 2022 onward
  • Fedora Server 31+
  • Debian, Ubuntu, and SUSE do not use XFS by default, but become exposed if an administrator manually selects XFS during installation, and reflink=1.

For those running affected distributions, we recommend that you apply the latest security updates via your distribution’s advisory and perform a system reboot.

How does RefluXFS work?

RefluXFS is a race condition in the Linux kernel’s XFS filesystem copy-on-write (CoW) path. It allows an unprivileged local user to overwrite the on-disk contents of any readable file on a reflink-enabled XFS volume.

The vulnerability triggers when two concurrent `O_DIRECT` writes target the same reflinked file. XFS uses CoW to handle writes to shared blocks: it allocates a new private block, remaps the file, and decrements the original block’s reference count. To avoid deadlocks, the kernel drops its inode lock while waiting for transaction log space. During this window, a second writer can complete its own CoW cycle, remapping the file to a new block and dropping the original block’s reference count to one.

When the first writer re-acquires the lock, it re-checks the reference count using a stale physical block address captured before the lock was dropped. Seeing a count of one, it incorrectly assumes the block is private and proceeds to write directly to the original file’s physical block. Because `O_DIRECT` bypasses kernel page cache and lacks revalidation, the write persists to disk, overwriting the target file. The change survives reboots, leaves no kernel log output, and bypasses standard file metadata checks.

Why Existing Mitigations Don’t Stop It

If you’re wondering whether your usual kernel hardening will catch this, the short answer is no, and it’s worth understanding why. The flaw lives at the filesystem allocation layer, which sits below or outside the reach of the defenses most systems rely on.

Memory-protection features like KASLR, SMEP, and SMAP are aimed at very different attack surfaces and simply don’t apply to block-layer writes. Kernel lockdown doesn’t help either, since it places no restrictions on O_DIRECT or FICLONE for unprivileged users. SELinux doesn’t block the affected path in testing, and seccomp profiles are no barrier as long as they permit write and ioctl, which ordinary profiles do.

The isolation mechanisms you might lean on in containerized environments fall short for the same underlying reason: user-namespace restrictions, container capability limits, and hardened allocators all operate at layers the flaw never touches. Because the vulnerability works through normal filesystem allocation, it slips past defenses designed to police memory, system calls, and privilege boundaries.

Required Action: This isn’t a vulnerability you can harden around, isolate, or live-patch. SELinux, container boundaries, memory protections, and even active threat monitoring all fall short because the flaw operates at the filesystem allocation layer, below every traditional defense. Immediate kernel patching and a full reboot are the only reliable mitigations.

Technical Details of the RefluXFS vulnerability:

You can find the technical details of this vulnerability at: https://cdn2.qualys.com/advisory/2026/07/22/RefluXFS.txt

Acknowledgments

We thank the XFS maintainers and the Linux kernel security team, particularly Carlos Maiolino, Greg Kroah-Hartman, Darrick J. Wong, Linus Torvalds, David Woodhouse, and Willy Tarreau, for their prompt coordination and rigorous patching efforts. We also thank the Anthropic team for their continued collaboration and thoughtful engagement throughout this work.

We extend our appreciation to the linux-distros mailing list community, particularly Sam James, Caryl Takvorian, Denis Pilipchuk, Salvatore Bonaccorso, and Solar Designer, for their constructive feedback during the coordinated disclosure process.

Qualys QID Coverage for Detecting the RefluXFS vulnerability (CVE-2026-64600):

Qualys is releasing the QIDs in the table below as they become available.

Qualys customers can use QID 45097 – Linux Kernel Version Running to identify the Linux kernel version active on the system at the time of the scan.

Immediate Action Required: Scan all Linux endpoints using QID(s). Prioritize patching for internet-facing assets.

Please check the Qualys Vulnerability Knowledgebase for the full list of coverage for this vulnerability.

Conclusion

RefluXFS demonstrates the value of applying advanced technology within a disciplined, expert-led security research process. The significance lies not in the technology alone, but in how it can help researchers identify serious flaws earlier, validate their impact, and support coordinated disclosure before attackers can exploit them. A resilient security posture ultimately depends on continuous visibility and timely action: we encourage customers to discover and understand exposed assets via CSAM and VMDR to identify and prioritize vulnerabilities across those assets, and use Patch Management to remediate them before the exploitation window closes.

Find out more about Qualys’ participation in Anthropic’s Project Glasswing.

Our security researchers then took over: we reviewed the model’s reasoning, reproduced the exploit, and independently verified every technical claim before coordinating disclosure with upstream maintainers. The model also produced an initial draft of the advisory, which our team validated and corrected against our own testing. This human-validated, AI-accelerated approach let us surface a complex kernel race condition while holding to the strict accuracy and responsible-disclosure standards expected; every finding here cleared the same evidence bar we apply to any Qualys security advisory.

sentences.en: For this research, we tasked Claude Mythos Preview with hunting for a Dirty COW–style race condition in the Linux kernel, iteratively refining our prompts to narrow its focus toward race conditions in the core memory-management and filesystem directories.
sentences.zh: 在这项研究中，我们给 Claude Mythos Preview 的任务是在 Linux 内核中寻找 Dirty COW 式的竞争条件，迭代地改进我们的提示，将其焦点缩小到核心内存管理和文件系统目录中的竞争条件。

sentences.en: After several iterations, the model identified a race condition in the XFS filesystem and generated a functional proof-of-concept local privilege escalation.
sentences.zh: 经过几次迭代后，该模型识别出 XFS 文件系统中的竞争条件，并生成了本地权限升级的功能概念验证。

sentences.en: Our security researchers then took over: we reviewed the model’s reasoning, reproduced the exploit, and independently verified every technical claim before coordinating disclosure with upstream maintainers.
sentences.zh: 然后我们的安全研究人员接手：我们审查了模型的推理，重现了漏洞，并在与上游维护人员协调披露之前独立验证了每项技术声明。

sentences.en: The model also produced an initial draft of the advisory, which our team validated and corrected against our own testing.
sentences.zh: 该模型还生成了该建议的初稿，我们的团队根据我们自己的测试对其进行了验证和纠正。

sentences.en: This human-validated, AI-accelerated approach let us surface a complex kernel race condition while holding to the strict accuracy and responsible-disclosure standards expected; every finding here cleared the same evidence bar we apply to any Qualys security advisory.
sentences.zh: 这种经过人工验证、人工智能加速的方法让我们能够揭示复杂的内核竞争条件，同时遵守预期的严格准确性和负责任的披露标准；这里的每一项发现都清除了我们应用于任何 Qualys 安全建议的相同证据栏。

blocks.html_en: Understanding the Potential Impact of the RefluXFS Vulnerability

sentences.en: Understanding the Potential Impact of the RefluXFS Vulnerability
sentences.zh: 了解 RefluXFS 漏洞的潜在影响

blocks.html_en: An unprivileged local user can overwrite the on-disk contents of any readable file on a reflink-enabled XFS volume. Our proof-of-concept reliably gains host root privileges by surgically modifying /etc/passwd or SUID-root binaries. Changes persist across reboots, leave no kernel log output, and bypass standard file metadata checks.

sentences.en: An unprivileged local user can overwrite the on-disk contents of any readable file on a reflink-enabled XFS volume.
sentences.zh: 非特权本地用户可以覆盖启用引用链接的 XFS 卷上任何可读文件的磁盘内容。

sentences.en: Our proof-of-concept reliably gains host root privileges by surgically modifying /etc/passwd or SUID-root binaries.
sentences.zh: 我们的概念验证通过手术修改 /etc/passwd 或 SUID-root 二进制文件可靠地获得主机 root 权限。

sentences.en: Changes persist across reboots, leave no kernel log output, and bypass standard file metadata checks.
sentences.zh: 更改在重新启动后仍然存在，不留下任何内核日志输出，并绕过标准文件元数据检查。

blocks.html_en: While CVE-2026-64600 itself is strictly local, successful exploitation on a compromised host could enable attackers to establish persistence, manipulate credentials, or facilitate lateral movement within a network.

sentences.en: While CVE-2026-64600 itself is strictly local, successful exploitation on a compromised host could enable attackers to establish persistence, manipulate credentials, or facilitate lateral movement within a network.
sentences.zh: 虽然 CVE-2026-64600 本身是严格本地的，但在受感染的主机上成功利用可能使攻击者能够建立持久性、操纵凭据或促进网络内的横向移动。

sentences.en: Watch a RefluXFS Proof-of-concept Demonstration
sentences.zh: 观看 RefluXFS 概念验证演示

blocks.html_en: The following proof-of-concept shown in the video below shows the RefluXFS vulnerability on a default RHEL 10.2 deployment. Starting as an unprivileged local user with no administrative rights, the exploit triggers the race condition to silently overwrite a protected system file at the block layer. Within seconds, the root account’s password protection is stripped, granting immediate, passwordless root access. The modification persists across reboots and leaves no kernel log artifacts.

sentences.en: The following proof-of-concept shown in the video below shows the RefluXFS vulnerability on a default RHEL 10.2 deployment.
sentences.zh: 下面视频中显示的概念验证显示了默认 RHEL 10.2 部署上的 RefluXFS 漏洞。

sentences.en: Starting as an unprivileged local user with no administrative rights, the exploit triggers the race condition to silently overwrite a protected system file at the block layer.
sentences.zh: 该漏洞从没有管理权限的非特权本地用户开始，触发竞争条件，以静默方式覆盖块层受保护的系统文件。

sentences.en: Within seconds, the root account’s password protection is stripped, granting immediate, passwordless root access.
sentences.zh: 几秒钟之内，root 帐户的密码保护就会被解除，从而授予立即、无密码的 root 访问权限。

sentences.en: The modification persists across reboots and leaves no kernel log artifacts.
sentences.zh: 修改在重新引导后仍然存在，并且不会留下任何内核日志工件。

blocks.html_en: RefluXFS PoC execution on RHEL 10.2. Unprivileged user escalates to root by exploiting the XFS reflink race condition.

sentences.en: RefluXFS PoC execution on RHEL 10.2.
sentences.zh: RefluXFS PoC 在 RHEL 10.2 上执行。

sentences.en: Unprivileged user escalates to root by exploiting the XFS reflink race condition.
sentences.zh: 非特权用户通过利用 XFS 重新链接竞争条件升级为 root。

sentences.zh: 漏洞范围和影响：

blocks.html_en: The RefluXFS vulnerability affects systems meeting all three of the following criteria:

sentences.en: The RefluXFS vulnerability affects systems meeting all three of the following criteria:
sentences.zh: RefluXFS 漏洞影响满足以下所有三个标准的系统：

blocks.html_en: • Kernel Version: Running Linux kernel v4.11 or later (2017) without the specific security patch.

sentences.en: Kernel Version: Running Linux kernel v4.11 or later (2017) without the specific security patch.
sentences.zh: 内核版本：运行 Linux 内核 v4.11 或更高版本（2017），没有特定的安全补丁。

blocks.html_en: • Filesystem Configuration: Utilizing an XFS filesystem with reflink=1 in its superblock.

sentences.en: Filesystem Configuration: Utilizing an XFS filesystem with reflink=1 in its superblock.
sentences.zh: 文件系统配置：使用超级块中 reflink=1 的 XFS 文件系统。

blocks.html_en: • File Structure: The filesystem must contain both a high-value target (a root-owned configuration file or SUID-root binary) and a directory writable by an unprivileged local user.

sentences.en: File Structure: The filesystem must contain both a high-value target (a root-owned configuration file or SUID-root binary) and a directory writable by an unprivileged local user.
sentences.zh: 文件结构：文件系统必须包含高价值目标（root 拥有的配置文件或 SUID-root 二进制文件）和非特权本地用户可写的目录。

sentences.en: How does RefluXFS work?
sentences.zh: RefluXFS 如何工作？

blocks.html_en: RefluXFS is a race condition in the Linux kernel’s XFS filesystem copy-on-write (CoW) path. It allows an unprivileged local user to overwrite the on-disk contents of any readable file on a reflink-enabled XFS volume.

sentences.en: RefluXFS is a race condition in the Linux kernel’s XFS filesystem copy-on-write (CoW) path.
sentences.zh: RefluXFS 是 Linux 内核的 XFS 文件系统写时复制 (CoW) 路径中的竞争条件。

sentences.en: It allows an unprivileged local user to overwrite the on-disk contents of any readable file on a reflink-enabled XFS volume.
sentences.zh: 它允许非特权本地用户覆盖启用引用链接的 XFS 卷上任何可读文件的磁盘内容。

blocks.html_en: The vulnerability triggers when two concurrent `O_DIRECT` writes target the same reflinked file. XFS uses CoW to handle writes to shared blocks: it allocates a new private block, remaps the file, and decrements the original block’s reference count. To avoid deadlocks, the kernel drops its inode lock while waiting for transaction log space. During this window, a second writer can complete its own CoW cycle, remapping the file to a new block and dropping the original block’s reference count to one.

sentences.en: The vulnerability triggers when two concurrent `O_DIRECT` writes target the same reflinked file.
sentences.zh: 当两个并发的“O_DIRECT”写入针对同一个引用链接文件时，就会触发该漏洞。

sentences.en: XFS uses CoW to handle writes to shared blocks: it allocates a new private block, remaps the file, and decrements the original block’s reference count.
sentences.zh: XFS 使用 CoW 处理对共享块的写入：它分配一个新的私有块，重新映射文​​件，并减少原始块的引用计数。

sentences.en: To avoid deadlocks, the kernel drops its inode lock while waiting for transaction log space.
sentences.zh: 为了避免死锁，内核在等待事务日志空间时会删除其索引节点锁。

sentences.en: During this window, a second writer can complete its own CoW cycle, remapping the file to a new block and dropping the original block’s reference count to one.
sentences.zh: 在此窗口期间，第二个写入器可以完成自己的 CoW 周期，将文件重新映射到新块并将原始块的引用计数降至 1。

blocks.html_en: When the first writer re-acquires the lock, it re-checks the reference count using a stale physical block address captured before the lock was dropped. Seeing a count of one, it incorrectly assumes the block is private and proceeds to write directly to the original file’s physical block. Because `O_DIRECT` bypasses kernel page cache and lacks revalidation, the write persists to disk, overwriting the target file. The change survives reboots, leaves no kernel log output, and bypasses standard file metadata checks.

sentences.en: When the first writer re-acquires the lock, it re-checks the reference count using a stale physical block address captured before the lock was dropped.
sentences.zh: 当第一个写入器重新获取锁时，它会使用在释放锁之前捕获的陈旧物理块地址重新检查引用计数。

sentences.en: Seeing a count of one, it incorrectly assumes the block is private and proceeds to write directly to the original file’s physical block.
sentences.zh: 当计数为一时，它错误地认为该块是私有的，并继续直接写入原始文件的物理块。

sentences.en: Because `O_DIRECT` bypasses kernel page cache and lacks revalidation, the write persists to disk, overwriting the target file.
sentences.zh: 由于“O_DIRECT”绕过内核页面缓存并且缺乏重新验证，因此写入会持续到磁盘，从而覆盖目标文件。

sentences.en: The change survives reboots, leaves no kernel log output, and bypasses standard file metadata checks.
sentences.zh: 该更改在重新启动后仍然存在，不会留下任何内核日志输出，并绕过标准文件元数据检查。

sentences.en: Why Existing Mitigations Don’t Stop It
sentences.zh: 为什么现有的缓解措施无法阻止它

blocks.html_en: If you’re wondering whether your usual kernel hardening will catch this, the short answer is no, and it’s worth understanding why. The flaw lives at the filesystem allocation layer, which sits below or outside the reach of the defenses most systems rely on.

sentences.en: If you’re wondering whether your usual kernel hardening will catch this, the short answer is no, and it’s worth understanding why.
sentences.zh: 如果你想知道你通常的内核强化是否会解决这个问题，简单的答案是否定的，并且值得理解为什么。

sentences.en: The flaw lives at the filesystem allocation layer, which sits below or outside the reach of the defenses most systems rely on.
sentences.zh: 该缺陷存在于文件系统分配层，该层位于大多数系统所依赖的防御的下方或之外。

blocks.html_en: Memory-protection features like KASLR, SMEP, and SMAP are aimed at very different attack surfaces and simply don’t apply to block-layer writes. Kernel lockdown doesn’t help either, since it places no restrictions on O_DIRECT or FICLONE for unprivileged users. SELinux doesn’t block the affected path in testing, and seccomp profiles are no barrier as long as they permit write and ioctl, which ordinary profiles do.

sentences.en: Memory-protection features like KASLR, SMEP, and SMAP are aimed at very different attack surfaces and simply don’t apply to block-layer writes.
sentences.zh: KASLR、SMEP 和 SMAP 等内存保护功能针对截然不同的攻击面，根本不适用于块层写入。

sentences.en: Kernel lockdown doesn’t help either, since it places no restrictions on O_DIRECT or FICLONE for unprivileged users.
sentences.zh: 内核锁定也没有帮助，因为它对非特权用户的 O_DIRECT 或 FICLONE 没有限制。

sentences.en: SELinux doesn’t block the affected path in testing, and seccomp profiles are no barrier as long as they permit write and ioctl, which ordinary profiles do.
sentences.zh: SELinux 不会在测试中阻止受影响的路径，只要 seccomp 配置文件允许写入和 ioctl（普通配置文件会这样做），那么它们就不是障碍。

blocks.html_en: The isolation mechanisms you might lean on in containerized environments fall short for the same underlying reason: user-namespace restrictions, container capability limits, and hardened allocators all operate at layers the flaw never touches. Because the vulnerability works through normal filesystem allocation, it slips past defenses designed to police memory, system calls, and privilege boundaries.

sentences.en: The isolation mechanisms you might lean on in containerized environments fall short for the same underlying reason: user-namespace restrictions, container capability limits, and hardened allocators all operate at layers the flaw never touches.
sentences.zh: 您在容器化环境中可能依赖的隔离机制由于相同的根本原因而无法实现：用户命名空间限制、容器功能限制和强化分配器都在缺陷从未触及的层上运行。

sentences.en: Because the vulnerability works through normal filesystem allocation, it slips past defenses designed to police memory, system calls, and privilege boundaries.
sentences.zh: 由于该漏洞通过正常的文件系统分配起作用，因此它绕过了旨在监管内存、系统调用和特权边界的防御措施。

blocks.html_en: Required Action: This isn’t a vulnerability you can harden around, isolate, or live-patch. SELinux, container boundaries, memory protections, and even active threat monitoring all fall short because the flaw operates at the filesystem allocation layer, below every traditional defense. Immediate kernel patching and a full reboot are the only reliable mitigations.

sentences.en: Required Action: This isn’t a vulnerability you can harden around, isolate, or live-patch.
sentences.zh: 所需的操作：这不是一个可以强化、隔离或实时修补的漏洞。

sentences.en: SELinux, container boundaries, memory protections, and even active threat monitoring all fall short because the flaw operates at the filesystem allocation layer, below every traditional defense.
sentences.zh: SELinux、容器边界、内存保护，甚至主动威胁监控都无法实现，因为该缺陷在文件系统分配层运行，低于所有传统防御措施。

sentences.en: Immediate kernel patching and a full reboot are the only reliable mitigations.
sentences.zh: 立即修补内核并完全重新启动是唯一可靠的缓解措施。

blocks.html_en: RefluXFS demonstrates the value of applying advanced technology within a disciplined, expert-led security research process. The significance lies not in the technology alone, but in how it can help researchers identify serious flaws earlier, validate their impact, and support coordinated disclosure before attackers can exploit them. A resilient security posture ultimately depends on continuous visibility and timely action: we encourage customers to discover and understand exposed assets via CSAM and VMDR to identify and prioritize vulnerabilities across those assets, and use Patch Management to remediate them before the exploitation window closes.

content_html_zh:
执行摘要

Qualys 威胁研究单位 (TRU) 发现了 CVE-2026-64600，这是 Linux 内核的 XFS 文件系统写时复制路径中的竞争条件。 拥有普通本地帐户的攻击者可以利用此竞争条件覆盖磁盘上受保护的文件并获得受影响系统上的主机 root 权限，包括在强制模式下运行 SELinux 的部署。

这一发现源自 Qualys 和 Anthropic 之间的一项结构化研究计划，其中我们将 Claude Mythos Preview 集成到我们的手动审核工作流程中，以加速我们的研究，同时保持严格的人工监督。

利用此漏洞，以普通非特权用户身份运行的进程可以触发该缺陷，并能够在块层覆盖 XFS 卷上的任何可读文件。 该原语直接转换为主机 root 权限。 漏洞利用高度可靠，不会留下任何内核日志输出。 该漏洞会影响任何启用了 reflink 的 XFS 根文件系统的 Linux 发行版。 这包括主要企业平台的默认安装，例如 RHEL、Oracle Linux、Amazon Linux 和 Fedora。

该漏洞自内核版本 4.11（大约 2017 年）以来就已存在，根据 Qualys Cyber​​Security Asset Management 的分析，该漏洞可能会影响全球超过 1640 万个系统，由于其高可利用性和缺乏内核日志记录，需要立即引起关注。

建议立即修补内核以消除此漏洞。 在标准强化设置下，漏洞利用始终成功，并且磁盘上的修改在系统重新启动后仍然存在。

供应商固定内核现已可用，并向后移植到企业发行版。 组织应优先修补暴露的多租户系统，并确保重新启动以验证更新。 截至目前，还没有可靠或实用的缓解措施或临时配置更改可用。

我们将此视为紧急优先事项，因为利用可以从普通的本地特权开始。 该漏洞存在于标准企业内核构建中，成功利用该漏洞可提供主机根权限。 该漏洞利用在常见的内核强化设置下工作，并且可以使用固定内核。

RefluXFS 更关心的是人工智能如何负责任地使用，而不是人工智能能做什么。 有能力的模型已经广泛使用一段时间了；限制因素是方向，而不是能力。 教训并不是人工智能可以针对软件来产生漏洞；而是人工智能可以针对软件进行攻击。人工智能与专家判断相结合，可以帮助防御者在被滥用之前发现并弥补严重缺陷。

发现过程和研究方法

在这项研究中，我们给 Claude Mythos Preview 的任务是在 Linux 内核中寻找 Dirty COW 式的竞争条件，迭代地改进我们的提示，将其焦点缩小到核心内存管理和文件系统目录中的竞争条件。 经过几次迭代后，该模型识别出 XFS 文件系统中的竞争条件，并生成了本地权限升级的功能概念验证。 然后我们的安全研究人员接手：我们审查了模型的推理，重现了漏洞，并在与上游维护人员协调披露之前独立验证了每项技术声明。 该模型还生成了该建议的初稿，我们的团队根据我们自己的测试对其进行了验证和纠正。 这种经过人工验证、人工智能加速的方法让我们能够揭示复杂的内核竞争条件，同时遵守预期的严格准确性和负责任的披露标准；这里的每一项发现都清除了我们应用于任何 Qualys 安全建议的相同证据栏。

了解有关 Qualys 参与 Anthropic 的 Glasswing 项目的更多信息。

了解 RefluXFS 漏洞的潜在影响

非特权本地用户可以覆盖启用引用链接的 XFS 卷上任何可读文件的磁盘内容。 我们的概念验证通过手术修改 /etc/passwd 或 SUID-root 二进制文件可靠地获得主机 root 权限。 更改在重新启动后仍然存在，不留下任何内核日志输出，并绕过标准文件元数据检查。

虽然 CVE-2026-64600 本身是严格本地的，但在受感染的主机上成功利用可能使攻击者能够建立持久性、操纵凭据或促进网络内的横向移动。

观看 RefluXFS 概念验证演示

下面视频中显示的概念验证显示了默认 RHEL 10.2 部署上的 RefluXFS 漏洞。 该漏洞从没有管理权限的非特权本地用户开始，触发竞争条件，以静默方式覆盖块层受保护的系统文件。 几秒钟之内，root 帐户的密码保护就会被解除，从而授予立即、无密码的 root 访问权限。 修改在重新引导后仍然存在，并且不会留下任何内核日志工件。

RefluXFS PoC 在 RHEL 10.2 上执行。 非特权用户通过利用 XFS 重新链接竞争条件升级为 root。

受影响的系统和先决条件

漏洞范围和影响：

RefluXFS 漏洞影响满足以下所有三个标准的系统：

  1. 内核版本：运行 Linux 内核 v4.11 或更高版本（2017），没有特定的安全补丁。
  2. 文件系统配置：使用超级块中 reflink=1 的 XFS 文件系统。
  3. 文件结构：文件系统必须包含高价值目标（root 拥有的配置文件或 SUID-root 二进制文件）和非特权本地用户可写的目录。

受影响的发行版：

自 2017 年以来，该漏洞已存在于每个主线和稳定内核中。 它不需要特殊功能或非默认配置。 下面列出的分布是已确认受到影响的分布；这不是一个详尽的列表，其他发行版，特别是源自 RHEL 的发行版，也可能容易受到攻击：

  • RHEL 8、9 和 10
  • CentOS Stream 8、9 和 10
  • Oracle Linux 8、9 和 10
  • Rocky 和 ​​AlmaLinux 8、9、10
  • CloudLinux 8、9 和 10
  • 从 2022 年 12 月起，Amazon Linux 2023 和 Amazon Linux 2 AMI
  • Fedora 服务器 31+
  • Debian、Ubuntu 和 SUSE 默认情况下不使用 XFS，但如果管理员在安装过程中手动选择 XFS 并且 reflink=1，则会暴露。

对于那些运行受影响的发行版的用户，我们建议您通过发行版的公告应用最新的安全更新并重新启动系统。

RefluXFS 如何工作？

RefluXFS 是 Linux 内核的 XFS 文件系统写时复制 (CoW) 路径中的竞争条件。 它允许非特权本地用户覆盖启用引用链接的 XFS 卷上任何可读文件的磁盘内容。

当两个并发的“O_DIRECT”写入针对同一个引用链接文件时，就会触发该漏洞。 XFS 使用 CoW 处理对共享块的写入：它分配一个新的私有块，重新映射文​​件，并减少原始块的引用计数。 为了避免死锁，内核在等待事务日志空间时会删除其索引节点锁。 在此窗口期间，第二个写入器可以完成自己的 CoW 周期，将文件重新映射到新块并将原始块的引用计数降至 1。

当第一个写入器重新获取锁时，它会使用在释放锁之前捕获的陈旧物理块地址重新检查引用计数。 当计数为一时，它错误地认为该块是私有的，并继续直接写入原始文件的物理块。 由于“O_DIRECT”绕过内核页面缓存并且缺乏重新验证，因此写入会持续到磁盘，从而覆盖目标文件。 该更改在重新启动后仍然存在，不会留下任何内核日志输出，并绕过标准文件元数据检查。

为什么现有的缓解措施无法阻止它

如果你想知道你通常的内核强化是否会解决这个问题，简单的答案是否定的，并且值得理解为什么。 该缺陷存在于文件系统分配层，该层位于大多数系统所依赖的防御的下方或之外。

KASLR、SMEP 和 SMAP 等内存保护功能针对截然不同的攻击面，根本不适用于块层写入。 内核锁定也没有帮助，因为它对非特权用户的 O_DIRECT 或 FICLONE 没有限制。 SELinux 不会在测试中阻止受影响的路径，只要 seccomp 配置文件允许写入和 ioctl（普通配置文件会这样做），那么它们就不是障碍。

您在容器化环境中可能依赖的隔离机制由于相同的根本原因而无法实现：用户命名空间限制、容器功能限制和强化分配器都在缺陷从未触及的层上运行。 由于该漏洞通过正常的文件系统分配起作用，因此它绕过了旨在监管内存、系统调用和特权边界的防御措施。

所需的操作：这不是一个可以强化、隔离或实时修补的漏洞。 SELinux、容器边界、内存保护，甚至主动威胁监控都无法实现，因为该缺陷在文件系统分配层运行，低于所有传统防御措施。 立即修补内核并完全重新启动是唯一可靠的缓解措施。

结论

RefluXFS 展示了在严格的、专家主导的安全研究过程中应用先进技术的价值。 其重要性不仅在于技术本身，还在于它如何帮助研究人员更早地识别严重缺陷，验证其影响，并在攻击者利用这些缺陷之前支持协调披露。 弹性安全态势最终取决于持续的可见性和及时的行动：我们鼓励客户通过 CSAM 和 VMDR 发现和了解暴露的资产，以识别这些资产中的漏洞并确定优先级，并使用补丁管理在漏洞利用窗口关闭之前修复这些漏洞。
